description: v7 correlation surface: agent.id × agent.instance_id × sidecar.instance_id, identity churn, on-demand agent discovery confidence, continuous AI inventory rollups, and per-connector header-presence rate.

panels.title: Active agent.id (5m)
panels.description: Distinct gen_ai_agent_id values that emitted a token in the last 5 minutes. Should match the number of installed connectors that are actively receiving traffic.

panels.title: Active agent_instance_id (5m, Loki)
panels.description: Distinct defenseclaw_agent_instance_id values seen on gateway events in the last 5 minutes. agent_instance_id lives on log attributes (not on metric labels — keeps Prometheus cardinality bounded) so we read it from Loki. A single connector running multiple shells will show >1 here.

panels.title: Sidecar instances (5m)
panels.description: Distinct sidecar.instance_id values seen on gateway events. Should be exactly 1 unless you are running multiple sidecars on the same host.

panels.title: AI discovery active signals
panels.description: Most recent active-signal count from continuous AI visibility scans.

panels.title: Components observed
panels.description: Distinct (ecosystem, name) components scored by the two-axis Bayesian engine.

options.content: Identity health is a function of *traffic*. The two left panels populate as soon as any connector posts OTLP or fires a hook. The two right panels populate once an LLM call flows through the sidecar — they're sourced from Loki because `gen_ai.agent.id` is a log/event attribute (kept off Prometheus labels to bound cardinality).

panels.title: OTLP records by connector (source)
panels.description: Inbound OTLP-JSON records grouped by the `source` label (= connector name). Populates as soon as a connector starts posting to /v1/logs|metrics|traces. Empty == no connector wired to the local OTel collector yet.

panels.title: Hook invocations by connector
panels.description: Connector hook fires (`defenseclaw_connector_hook_invocations_total`) — `connector` label is the canonical name. Populates as soon as a hook script (Codex, Claude Code, Hermes, Cursor, Windsurf, GeminiCLI, Copilot) fires.

panels.title: Distinct agent.id observed (Loki, by gen_ai.agent.name)
panels.description: Loki-derived count of distinct `gen_ai_agent_id` values per connector observed in event logs. Populates only when LLM events flow through the sidecar. Sustained growth on the same `gen_ai_agent_name` = the connector is rotating its agent.id (instability — should be stable per install).

targets.expr: count by (gen_ai_agent_name) (sum by (gen_ai_agent_name, gen_ai_agent_id) (count_over_time({service_namespace=~"defenseclaw|ai-governance"} | json | __error__="" | gen_ai_agent_id != "" [$__interval])))

panels.title: Header-presence rate (Loki) — events with gen_ai.agent.id set
panels.description: Loki-derived: fraction of gateway log events (per connector via `gen_ai_agent_name`) that also carry `gen_ai_agent_id` (i.e. the `X-DefenseClaw-Agent-Id` header was honored end-to-end). 1.0 = healthy, below 1.0 = the connector is dropping identity on some calls.

targets.expr: sum by (gen_ai_agent_name) (rate({service_namespace=~"defenseclaw|ai-governance"} | json | __error__="" | gen_ai_agent_id != "" [$__interval])) / sum by (gen_ai_agent_name) (rate({service_namespace=~"defenseclaw|ai-governance"} | json | gen_ai_agent_name != "" [$__interval]))

options.content: These panels track on-demand agent discovery runs (POST /agent/discovery). Run **`defenseclaw agent discover`** from any host with the CLI installed and reports will start landing here.

panels.title: Discovery runs by source × cache_hit × result
panels.description: Per-minute rate of accepted on-demand discovery reports, broken down by report `source` (cli/api), whether the CLI hit its local cache, and the gateway's accept verdict (`ok`/`malformed`/`rejected`).

defaults.noValue: no on-demand discoveries yet — run `defenseclaw agent discover`

panels.title: Discovery duration p50/p95
panels.description: Wall-clock time per on-demand discovery scan, sourced from `defenseclaw_agent_discovery_duration_milliseconds`. Sustained p95 > 1s usually means a connector probe is timing out — cross-reference the errors table.

panels.title: Per-connector install state (latest)
panels.description: 1=installed, 0=not installed. Sourced from `defenseclaw_agent_discovery_installed_ratio` (the gauge `defenseclaw.agent.discovery.installed` is unitless, so the OTel-Prometheus exporter appends the conventional `_ratio` suffix). Empty until at least one CLI discovery report lands.

panels.title: Discovery errors by connector × reason (1h)
panels.description: Bounded error reasons emitted by `defenseclaw.agent.discovery.errors` (e.g. version-probe timeout, parse failure). Empty == clean discovery runs.

panels.title: AI signals by category
panels.description: supported_connector / ai_cli / ai_lib / ai_extension / etc. — the bounded taxonomy continuous AI discovery emits.

panels.title: Identity confidence distribution
panels.description: Two-axis Bayesian engine identity_score [0,1] across all components. Cluster near 1.0 = strong identity (we're sure what this is). Cluster near 0 = ambiguous components — usually means the detector saw a signal that overlaps with multiple known products.

panels.title: Presence confidence distribution
panels.description: presence_score [0,1] — how confident the engine is that the component is currently installed. very_low presence with high identity is the 'SDK was removed but manifest still around' signature.

title: DefenseClaw — Agent identity
